sentence: A useful theory does more than explain what has already happened; it also helps us ask better questions about what may happen next.
reference: 一个有用的理论不仅能解释已经发生的事情，还能帮助我们针对接下来可能发生的事情提出更好的问题。
deductions: 没有译出分号后的递进关系
把 may 误译成必然

sentence: The value of disagreement lies partly in its ability to reveal assumptions that would otherwise remain unnoticed.
reference: 分歧的价值，部分在于它能够揭示那些原本不会被注意到的假设。
deductions: lie in 误译为位于而非在于

items.sourceText: Tracing the history of the term, we can see how the definition of scientific literacy has shifted over time, muddying the waters when it comes to determining the goals of science education.
items.referenceTranslation: 追溯该术语的历史，我们可以看到科学素养的定义如何随时间推移而变化，这使得对科学教育目标的确定变得模糊不清。
keyPoints: Tracing the history of the term 作状语
muddying the waters 表示“使问题变得不清楚”

phrases.meaning: 使问题复杂化；使情况不明朗

sentenceAnalysis.sentence: Tracing the history of the term, we can see how the definition of scientific literacy has shifted over time, muddying the waters when it comes to determining the goals of science education.
sentenceAnalysis.grammarBreakdown: Tracing... 是现在分词短语作状语；how 引导宾语从句；muddying... 是结果状语。
sentenceAnalysis.translation: 追溯该术语的历史，我们可以看到科学素养的定义如何随时间推移而变化，这使得对科学教育目标的确定变得模糊不清。

commonMistakes: 没有处理 when it comes to 的语义

items.sourceText: A return to that version of scientific literacy, which focused more on teaching what science is and how it works and less on memorizing scientific facts, seems like something society today desperately needs.
items.referenceTranslation: 回归那种更侧重于教授科学的本质与运作方式，而非死记科学事实的科学素养理念，似乎是当今社会迫切需要的。
keyPoints: which 引导非限制性定语从句
what science is and how it works 是并列宾语从句

sentenceAnalysis.sentence: A return to that version of scientific literacy, which focused more on teaching what science is and how it works and less on memorizing scientific facts, seems like something society today desperately needs.
sentenceAnalysis.grammarBreakdown: which 从句解释 that version；what 和 how 引导并列宾语从句；society today desperately needs 修饰 something。
sentenceAnalysis.translation: 回归那种更侧重于教授科学的本质与运作方式，而非死记科学事实的科学素养理念，似乎是当今社会迫切需要的。

commonMistakes: which 从句层次处理不清

items.sourceText: Educators advanced the idea of having students complete detailed laboratory exercises in high schools in the belief that such work was beneficial primarily as a way to enhance logical reasoning and observational skills.
items.referenceTranslation: 教育家们曾提出让学生在高中阶段完成详细的实验训练的想法，他们认为这种工作主要有利于增强逻辑推理和观察能力。
keyPoints: advanced the idea 表示“提出想法”
of having students complete... 修饰 idea
that 引导 belief 的同位语从句

sentenceAnalysis.sentence: Educators advanced the idea of having students complete detailed laboratory exercises in high schools in the belief that such work was beneficial primarily as a way to enhance logical reasoning and observational skills.
sentenceAnalysis.grammarBreakdown: of having... 作 idea 的后置说明；in the belief that... 表示“基于某种认识”；to enhance... 表目的。
sentenceAnalysis.translation: 教育家们曾提出让学生在高中阶段完成详细的实验训练的想法，他们认为这种工作主要有利于增强逻辑推理和观察能力。

commonMistakes: belief 后 that 从句关系不清

items.sourceText: It wasn't until the phrase "scientific literacy" came along in the 1940s that science had the formidable slogan it needed to command public attention and make improving science education an important national goal.
items.referenceTranslation: 直到 20 世纪 40 年代“科学素养”这一说法出现后，科学才有了其所需的响亮口号，从而引起公众的关注，并使改进科学教育成为重要的国家目标。
keyPoints: It wasn't until...that... 是强调句
to command... and make... 表目的

sentenceAnalysis.sentence: It wasn't until the phrase "scientific literacy" came along in the 1940s that science had the formidable slogan it needed to command public attention and make improving science education an important national goal.
sentenceAnalysis.coreStructure: It wasn't until... that science had the slogan.
sentenceAnalysis.grammarBreakdown: 强调句突出时间；it needed 修饰 slogan；to command 和 make 并列。
sentenceAnalysis.translation: 直到 20 世纪 40 年代“科学素养”这一说法出现后，科学才有了其所需的响亮口号，从而引起公众的关注，并使改进科学教育成为重要的国家目标。

commonMistakes: make 后宾补结构处理错误

items.sourceText: The intense focus on scientific literacy in the United States originally grew out of the critical role of science and technology during World War II, as well as the perceived deficiencies of American soldiers.
items.referenceTranslation: 美国对科学素养的高度关注，最初源于科学技术在二战中的关键作用，以及当时对美国士兵能力不足的认识。
keyPoints: grew out of 表示“源于”
as well as 连接两个来源
perceived deficiencies 表示“被认为存在的不足”

sentenceAnalysis.sentence: The intense focus on scientific literacy in the United States originally grew out of the critical role of science and technology during World War II, as well as the perceived deficiencies of American soldiers.
sentenceAnalysis.coreStructure: The intense focus ... grew out of...
sentenceAnalysis.grammarBreakdown: 主语较长；grew out of 后接两个并列来源；as well as 连接 critical role 和 perceived deficiencies。
sentenceAnalysis.translation: 美国对科学素养的高度关注，最初源于科学技术在二战中的关键作用，以及当时对美国士兵能力不足的认识。

commonMistakes: as well as 并列关系漏译

items.sentence: They sometimes travel more than sixty miles to find food or water, and are very good at working out where other elephants are—even when they are out of sight.
items.reference: 它们有时跋涉六十多英里寻找食物或水，并且非常善于判断其他大象的位置，即使那些大象不在视线范围内。
items.longSentence: and 连接两个并列谓语；where 引导宾语从句；even when 引导让步状语从句。
deductions: work out 误译为工作出来
没有处理 even when 的让步语气

items.sentence: The researchers are convinced that the elephants always know precisely where they are in relation to all the resources they need, and can therefore take shortcuts, as well as following familiar routes.
items.reference: 研究人员确信，大象总是准确知道自己相对于所有所需资源的位置，因此既能走捷径，也能沿着熟悉的路线前进。
items.longSentence: that 引导宾语从句；where 引导 know 的宾语从句；and can therefore 与 know 并列。
deductions: in relation to 关系处理错误
as well as 误译成因果

items.sentence: One possibility was that they merely used their eyes and tried out the plants they found, but that would probably result in a lot of wasted time and energy, not least because their eyesight is actually not very good.
items.reference: 有一种可能性是，它们只是用眼睛观察并尝试所发现的植物，但那很可能会浪费大量时间和精力，尤其是因为它们的视力实际上并不好。
items.longSentence: 第一个 that 是表语从句；but 后形成转折；because 引导原因状语从句。
deductions: they found 作定语修饰 plants 未处理

items.sentence: The volatile chemicals produced by plants can be carried a long way, and they are very characteristic: each plant or tree has its own particular odor signature.
items.reference: 植物产生的挥发性化学物质可以被携带到很远的地方，而且它们非常具有特征性：每种植物或树木都有自己独特的气味标识。
items.longSentence: produced by plants 是后置定语；冒号后解释 characteristic 的具体含义。
deductions: 冒号后的解释关系漏译

items.sentence: The experiment showed that elephants may well use smell to identify patches of trees that are good to eat, and secondly to assess the quality of the trees within each patch.
items.reference: 实验表明，大象很可能利用嗅觉来识别适合食用的树丛，并进一步评估每片树丛中树木的质量。
items.longSentence: that 引导宾语从句；两个 to do 结构并列表示 use smell 的两个目的。
deductions: secondly 后并列结构没有译出

notes: 2023 translation template only; content awaits manual entry.
2022 translation template only; content awaits manual entry.
2021 translation template only; content awaits manual entry.
